lines.gurmukhi: ਭੀ ਤੂੰਹੈ ਸਾਲਾਹਣਾ ਆਖਣ ਲਹੈ ਨ ਚਾਉ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: bhee toonhai saalaahanaa aakhan lahai na chaau |1|
lines.english: -even then, I would worship and adore You, and my longing to chant Your Praises would not decrease. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਰਬਤੁ ਸੁਇਨਾ ਰੁਪਾ ਹੋਵੈ ਹੀਰੇ ਲਾਲ ਜੜਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: parabat sueinaa rupaa hovai heere laal jarraau |
lines.english: if the mountains became gold and silver, studded with gems and jewels
First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਭਾਰ ਅਠਾਰਹ ਮੇਵਾ ਹੋਵੈ ਗਰੁੜਾ ਹੋਇ ਸੁਆਉ ॥
lines.translit: bhaar atthaarah mevaa hovai garurraa hoe suaau |
lines.english: If all the eighteen loads of vegetation became fruits,

lines.gurmukhi: ਚੰਦੁ ਸੂਰਜੁ ਦੁਇ ਫਿਰਦੇ ਰਖੀਅਹਿ ਨਿਹਚਲੁ ਹੋਵੈ ਥਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: chand sooraj due firade rakheeeh nihachal hovai thaau |
lines.english: and the growing grass became sweet rice; if I were able to stop the sun and the moon in their orbits and hold them perfectly steady

lines.gurmukhi: ਭੀ ਤੂੰਹੈ ਸਾਲਾਹਣਾ ਆਖਣ ਲਹੈ ਨ ਚਾਉ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: bhee toonhai saalaahanaa aakhan lahai na chaau |2|
lines.english: -even then, I would worship and adore You, and my longing to chant Your Praises would not decrease. ||2||
First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੇ ਦੇਹੈ ਦੁਖੁ ਲਾਈਐ ਪਾਪ ਗਰਹ ਦੁਇ ਰਾਹੁ ॥
lines.translit: je dehai dukh laaeeai paap garah due raahu |
lines.english: If my body were afflicted with pain, under the evil influence of unlucky stars;

lines.gurmukhi: ਭੀ ਤੂੰਹੈ ਸਾਲਾਹਣਾ ਆਖਣ ਲਹੈ ਨ ਚਾਉ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: bhee toonhai saalaahanaa aakhan lahai na chaau |3|
lines.english: -even if this were my condition, I would still worship and adore You, and my longing to chant Your Praises would not decrease. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਤੁ ਪੀਣੇ ਰਾਜੇ ਸਿਰੈ ਉਪਰਿ ਰਖੀਅਹਿ ਏਵੈ ਜਾਪੈ ਭਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: rat peene raaje sirai upar rakheeeh evai jaapai bhaau |
lines.english: and if the blood-sucking kings were to hold power over me

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੁਰਗੈ ਦੀਆ ਮੋਹਣੀਆ ਇਸਤਰੀਆ ਹੋਵਨਿ ਨਾਨਕ ਸਭੋ ਜਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: suragai deea mohaneea isatareea hovan naanak sabho jaau |
lines.english: and even if the enticing heavenly beauties were my wives, O Nanak-all this shall pass away!
First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਗੀ ਪਾਲਾ ਕਪੜੁ ਹੋਵੈ ਖਾਣਾ ਹੋਵੈ ਵਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: agee paalaa kaparr hovai khaanaa hovai vaau |
lines.english: If fire and ice were my clothes, and the wind was my food;
The foolish demon, who does evil deeds, does not know his Lord and Master.

lines.gurmukhi: ਭੀ ਤੂਹੈ ਸਾਲਾਹਣਾ ਆਖਣ ਲਹੈ ਨ ਚਾਉ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: bhee toohai saalaahanaa aakhan lahai na chaau |4|
lines.english: Even then, I would worship and adore You, and my longing to chant Your Praises would not decrease. ||4||
Pauree:
The strife of this world is evil; these struggles are consuming it.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋ ਕਹੀਐ ਦੇਵਾਨਾ ਆਪੁ ਨ ਪਛਾਣਈ ॥
lines.translit: so kaheeai devaanaa aap na pachhaanee |
lines.english: Call him a mad-man, if he does not understand himself.

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਹ ਦੋਵੈ ਇਕੁ ਜਾਣੈ ਸੋਈ ਸਿਝਸੀ ॥
lines.translit: raah dovai ik jaanai soee sijhasee |
lines.english: One who recognizes that all spiritual paths lead to the One shall be emancipated.
Without the Lord's Name, life is worthless. Through doubt, the people are being destroyed.
One who speaks lies shall fall into hell and burn.
In all the world, the most blessed and sanctified are those who remain absorbed in Truth.
First Mehl, Salok:
One who eliminates selfishness and conceit is redeemed in the Court of the Lord. ||9||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋ ਜੀਵਿਆ ਜਿਸੁ ਮਨਿ ਵਸਿਆ ਸੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: so jeeviaa jis man vasiaa soe |
lines.english: They alone are truly alive, whose minds are filled with the Lord.
everything they eat is impure.
those who merely live shall depart in dishonor;
O Nanak, no one else is truly alive;
O Nanak, they are deluded and defrauded.
Intoxicated with power and thrilled with wealth,
They delight in their pleasures, and dance about shamelessly.
Without the Lord's Name, they lose their honor and depart. ||1||
First Mehl:
if the True Lord does not abide within the mind?

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਿਆ ਮੇਵਾ ਕਿਆ ਘਿਉ ਗੁੜੁ ਮਿਠਾ ਕਿਆ ਮੈਦਾ ਕਿਆ ਮਾਸੁ ॥
lines.translit: kiaa mevaa kiaa ghiau gurr mitthaa kiaa maidaa kiaa maas |
lines.english: What good are fruits, what good is ghee, sweet jaggery, what good is flour, and what good is meat?
What good is food, and what good are clothes,

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਿਆ ਕਪੜੁ ਕਿਆ ਸੇਜ ਸੁਖਾਲੀ ਕੀਜਹਿ ਭੋਗ ਬਿਲਾਸ ॥
lines.translit: kiaa kaparr kiaa sej sukhaalee keejeh bhog bilaas |
lines.english: What good are clothes, and what good is a soft bed, to enjoy pleasures and sensual delights?

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਸਚੇ ਨਾਮ ਵਿਣੁ ਸਭੇ ਟੋਲ ਵਿਣਾਸੁ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: naanak sache naam vin sabhe ttol vinaas |2|
lines.english: O Nanak, without the True Name, all this paraphernalia shall disappear. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਿਆ ਲਸਕਰ ਕਿਆ ਨੇਬ ਖਵਾਸੀ ਆਵੈ ਮਹਲੀ ਵਾਸੁ ॥
lines.translit: kiaa lasakar kiaa neb khavaasee aavai mahalee vaas |
lines.english: What good is an army, and what good are soldiers, servants and mansions to live in?
Pride in one's status is like poison-holding it in your hand and eating it, you shall die.
Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾਤੀ ਦੈ ਕਿਆ ਹਥਿ ਸਚੁ ਪਰਖੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: jaatee dai kiaa hath sach parakheeai |
lines.english: What good is social class and status? Truthfulness is measured within.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਚੇ ਕੀ ਸਿਰਕਾਰ ਜੁਗੁ ਜੁਗੁ ਜਾਣੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: sache kee sirakaar jug jug jaaneeai |
lines.english: The True Lord's Sovereign Rule is known throughout the ages.
The Drummer, the Guru, has announced the Lord's meditation, through the Word of the Shabad.
By the Order of our Lord and Master, we have been brought into this world.
One who respects the Hukam of the Lord's Command is honored and respected in the Court of the Lord.
Some have mounted their horses in response, and others are saddling up.
Salok, First Mehl:
Some have tied up their bridles, and others have already ridden off. ||10||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਣੁ ਕੀਸਾਰਾ ਚਿਥਿਆ ਕਣੁ ਲਇਆ ਤਨੁ ਝਾੜਿ ॥
lines.translit: san keesaaraa chithiaa kan leaa tan jhaarr |
lines.english: The corn on the cob is put into the thresher, and the kernels are separated from the cobs.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾ ਪਕਾ ਤਾ ਕਟਿਆ ਰਹੀ ਸੁ ਪਲਰਿ ਵਾੜਿ ॥
lines.translit: jaa pakaa taa kattiaa rahee su palar vaarr |
lines.english: When the crop is ripe, then it is cut down; only the stalks are left standing.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੁਇ ਪੁੜ ਚਕੀ ਜੋੜਿ ਕੈ ਪੀਸਣ ਆਇ ਬਹਿਠੁ ॥
lines.translit: due purr chakee jorr kai peesan aae bahitth |
lines.english: Placing the kernels between the two mill-stones, people sit and grind the corn.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਦਰਿ ਰਹੇ ਸੁ ਉਬਰੇ ਨਾਨਕ ਅਜਬੁ ਡਿਠੁ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: jo dar rahe su ubare naanak ajab dditth |1|
lines.english: Those kernels which stick to the central axle are spared-Nanak has seen this wonderful vision! ||1||
First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਵੇਖੁ ਜਿ ਮਿਠਾ ਕਟਿਆ ਕਟਿ ਕੁਟਿ ਬਧਾ ਪਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: vekh ji mitthaa kattiaa katt kutt badhaa paae |
lines.english: Look, and see how the sugar-cane is cut down. After cutting away its branches, its feet are bound together into bundles,
